Devlog

Building with merjs

merjs is a Zig-native SSR web framework with file-based routing. We used it to build this site. Here's what we hit along the way — documented so you don't have to.

On this page
Package setup gotchasMissing files from the packageh.* DSL quirksRaw HTML pageslayout.zig pitfallsVerdict

1. Package setup gotchas

Getting merjs into a new project requires a valid build.zig.zon. Several fields are easy to get wrong:

Missing fingerprint
Zig 0.15 requires a fingerprint field in build.zig.zon. If missing, the compiler prints the expected value — paste it in.
.fingerprint = 0xf5794ba041c0e27d,
Invalid package name
Package names can't use @"..." escape syntax. Use a plain snake_case identifier instead.
- .name = .@"frontend",
+ .name = .turboapi_frontend,
URL needs a pinned git ref
The dependency URL must include a specific commit hash in the fragment (#abcdef...) or Zig won't fetch it reproducibly.
Missing .hash field
After adding the URL, run zig fetch --save to compute the content hash. Without it the build fails immediately.

2. Missing files from the package

The biggest time sink. The published merjs package at the pinned commit was missing two categories of files — neither included in .paths in build.zig.zon:

wasm/counter_config.zig not in package
The wasm/ directory is excluded from the package. The build system imports counter_config.zig and fails with FileNotFound. Fix: create the file manually in the Zig global cache from the GitHub source.
src/ runtime files missing
server.zig, ssr.zig, watcher.zig, prerender.zig, router.zig, request.zig, response.zig, static.zig, env.zig, html.zig, html_lint.zig, dhi.zig, worker.zig — all absent from the published package. Had to copy them manually from the Zig global cache after a failed build populated it.
📦If starting fresh: clone the repo directly rather than depending on it as a Zig package. The published package at the pinned hash is incomplete.

3. h.* DSL quirks

merjs provides a Zig HTML builder DSL. It's clean once you know the rules — but several are non-obvious:

Void elements take zero args
h.br(), h.hr(), h.input() take no arguments. Passing .{} causes "expected 0 args".
- h.br(.{})  // compile error
+ h.br()     // correct
Single child requires a tuple
Passing one child node directly fails. Wrap it in a single-element tuple:
- h.pre(.{}, h.code(.{}, "..."))
+ h.pre(.{}, .{h.code(.{}, "...")})
thead/tbody same rule
h.thead(.{}, h.tr(...)) fails for the same reason. Must be h.thead(.{}, .{h.tr(...)}).
h.pre with h.code inside
Hits the single-child tuple issue. For code blocks, pass a raw string to h.pre() directly — it renders in monospace anyway and avoids the nesting problem.

4. Raw HTML pages

For pages needing embedded JS (Chart.js, etc.), the h.* DSL is limiting. merjs lets you bypass layout.zig entirely with raw HTML responses:

pub fn render(req: mer.Request) mer.Response {
    _ = req;
    return .{
        .status    = .ok,
        .content_type = .html,
        .body      = html,
    };
}

const html =
    \\<!DOCTYPE html>
    \\...
;   // don't forget the semicolon
⚠️Raw HTML pages still need pub const meta: mer.Meta exported — even though meta isn't used. Missing it causes a compile error from the generated router. The closing semicolon after the multiline string is also easy to forget; the error message points to the last HTML line, which is confusing.

5. layout.zig pitfalls

layout.zig uses a Zig ArrayList writer pattern to build the HTML shell. Two things bit us:

CSS accidentally placed outside writeAll
When range-replacing inside layout.zig it's easy to insert CSS after the closing paren of writeAll("""). Zig compiles fine but the CSS never reaches the browser. Best fix: rewrite the whole file cleanly rather than patching small ranges.
404.zig duplicate HTML block
A bad range-replace left two concatenated versions of the page HTML in the string literal. The Zig compiler error ("expected ';' after declaration") pointed to a line mid-way through the HTML, which is confusing. Fix: find and delete the orphaned duplicate block.

Verdict

merjs is a genuinely good idea. Zig-native SSR with file-based routing, sub-millisecond builds, and a clean HTML DSL. The output is lean and the dev server is fast.

The main blocker right now is the incomplete package publish — the .paths list in build.zig.zon excludes wasm/ and most of src/, so fresh installs fail immediately. Once that's resolved, setup should be straightforward.

For JS-heavy pages, raw HTML is a clean escape hatch. The framework doesn't fight you. We'd use it again once the packaging story is sorted.

merjs on GitHub →TurboAPI docs
